events.text: Patient is a 35-year-old Black female with a BMI of 23.5. Diagnosed during pregnancy workup. No family history of cancer. BRCA1/2 negative.

At age 34, patient discovered a left breast lump during first trimester of pregnancy. Biopsy confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma (8500/3), left breast upper outer quadrant (C50.4), grade 3, ER negative, PR negative, HER2 IHC 3+, Ki-67 75%.
At age 34, left breast core biopsy: invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 3 (tubule score 3, nuclear grade 3, mitotic score 3). ER negative (
At age 34, breast ultrasound (mammogram deferred due to pregnancy) showed a 3.8 cm hypoechoic irregular mass in the left upper outer quadrant with suspicious ipsilateral axillary lymph nodes.
At age 34, MRI breast (non-contrast protocol during pregnancy) confirmed 4.0 cm mass with axillary adenopathy.
At age 34, stage IIIA (cT2N2M0) left breast invasive ductal carcinoma, HER2-positive, ER/PR-negative, diagnosed during pregnancy. Staging limited due to pregnancy.
At age 34, initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy during second trimester: doxorubicin 60 mg/m2 and cyclophosphamide (AC) every 21 days for 4 cycles. HER2-directed therapy deferred until after delivery.
At age 35, patient delivered a healthy infant at 37 weeks via planned cesarean section. Infant with normal birth weight, no complications.
At age 35, post-delivery, initiated taxane phase: paclitaxel 80 mg/m2 weekly for 12 weeks with trastuzumab and pertuzumab added.
At age 35, post-delivery staging CT and PET/CT showed no distant metastatic disease. Breast mass decreased to 1.5 cm after AC. No new sites of disease.
At age 35, underwent left modified radical mastectomy with axillary dissection. Pathologic complete response (pCR) achieved: ypT0 ypN0. No residual invasive or in situ disease.

At age 35, left mastectomy specimen: pathologic complete response. No residual invasive carcinoma. No residual DCIS. 0 of 18 axillary lymph nodes positive. Treatment bed with fibrosis and histiocytic reaction.
At age 35, received adjuvant chest wall and regional nodal radiation, 50 Gy in 25 fractions.
At age 35, given pCR, continued adjuvant trastuzumab and pertuzumab to complete 1 year of HER2-directed therapy.
At age 35, LVEF 62% baseline, remained stable at 60% throughout treatment. Tolerated well.
At age 34, during AC chemotherapy: grade 2 nausea, grade 2 alopecia, grade 1 fatigue. No febrile neutropenia episodes.

At age 35, during taxane/HP phase: grade 1 peripheral neuropathy, grade 1 diarrhea from pertuzumab. Mild infusion-related reaction with trastuzumab first dose (grade 1 chills, resolved with slowing infusion).

At age 35, completed all adjuvant therapy. Pathologic complete response achieved. Excellent prognosis. Surveillance plan: exam every 3 months, annual imaging. Not a candidate for endocrine therapy given ER-negative status. Contraception counseling provided.
At age 34, tumor profiling: ERBB2 amplification (copy number 16), TP53 Y220C, no additional actionable mutations. TMB 5 mutations/Mb. MSS.
At age 35, surveillance CT 6 months post-mastectomy showed no evidence of disease.

scenario_blurb: Metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer. IHC 3+ or FISH amplified. Treated with trastuzumab-based regimens (THP, T-DM1, trastuzumab deruxtecan). Include hormone receptor status variation (ER+/HER2+ and ER-/HER2+). Some with brain metastases.
